title: Carex subnivalis
taxonomy: Carex subnivalis is classified as a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae. It belongs to the genus Carex, which consists of approximately 2,000 species of sedges. Within the genus Carex, Carex subnivalis is further classified as a member of the subgenus Vignea. The species is native to the Himalayan region and is known for its ability to thrive in high-altitude environments. Carex subnivalis is often found growing in rocky areas and alpine meadows.

intro: Carex subnivalis, also known as subalpine sedge, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Carex subnivalis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Carex subnivalis is typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly consumed to alleviate digestive discomfort and promote overall well-being.
